column1: Analysis of Social Media Presence of Ethiopian Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs)

column3: Source
column9: Notes

column2: Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia
column3: Official X account linked from government page
column9: Account has verified badge, posts official government updates, widely referenced in news.

column3: Addis Ababa City Government / Mayor’s Office / Official website (addismayor.gov.et)
column9: Username matches official title, content includes government updates, high follower count, and aligns with official communication channels.

column3: FDRE Ministry of Education
column9: Account exists but lacks verification badge. No confirmed official Facebook presence found. Identity requires additional confirmation from government or ministry sources.

column3: Government communication channels (Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ethiopian Television)
column9: No verified personal Facebook account was identified for Taye Atske Selassie. Public updates, photos, and official communications about his activities are shared through government channels, including the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia and Ethiopian Television (ETV).

column3: Office of the Prime Minister official website (cross-referenced social media accounts)
column9: Social media accounts were cross-checked with official government website references and show consistent activity related to the individual’s role.

column2: Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia
column3: Official government communication channels (Ministry of Foreign Affairs, embassy pages)
column9: X account matches the individual’s identity and posts consistent official content. No personal Facebook account identified activities are communicated through official Ministry of Foreign Affairs and embassy Facebook pages.

column2: Minister of Irrigation and Lowlands of Ethiopia
column3: Official government records / credible public sources
column9: Current position confirmed through official government sources individual has held multiple senior federal roles including Minister of Defense and Minister of Innovation and Technology. An X account was identified, but the username and profile details do not clearly align with the individual’s official role, and no confirmation from authoritative sources was found.

column3: FDRE Ministry of Justice official website and social media channels
column9: No verified personal X (Twitter) or Facebook accounts were identified for Hanna Arayaselassie Zemichael. Public communications, activities, and official updates are disseminated through institutional channels, primarily the FDRE Ministry of Justice’s official social media accounts. A professional LinkedIn profile exists under her full name.

column3: Ministry of Health official communication channels and institutional platforms
column9: An X (Twitter) account was identified for Mekdes Daba under the handle @MekdesDaba, which reflects consistent professional activity aligned with her role as Minister of Health. No verified personal Facebook account was identified her activities are primarily shared through official Ministry of Health and related institutional Facebook pages.

column3: Official Facebook page and news media references (e.g., Addis Standard)
column9: No personal X account identified; mentions of the individual appear mainly through news and media sources rather than an official profile.

column3: Official Facebook page and public government communications
column9: No clearly identifiable or officially confirmed personal X account; presence on the platform appears fragmented and not linked to an authoritative source.

column3: Official Facebook page and regional government/media communication channels
column9: No confirmed personal X account; official updates appear through regional media outlets and government-affiliated communication channels.

column3: Official X(Twitter) account and regional government/media communication channels
column9: The verified X account is the primary official personal social media presence of Mustafa Mohammed Omer and is used for public communication, policy updates, and regional announcements. Facebook does not have a clearly designated verified personal account information related to the president is instead shared through official Somali Regional State communication channels and national media outlets.

column2: Member of Parliament, National Movement of Amhara (NaMA)
column3: Publicly referenced social media accounts and political media coverage
column9: Both accounts are associated with Dr. Desalegn Chane and are used for political communication and public engagement.

column3: Tigray Communication Affairs Bureau and Ethiopian Broadcasting Corporation (EBC)
column9: Lt. Gen. Tadesse Worede has no clearly identifiable or verified personal social media accounts. Official communications related to his role are primarily disseminated through government and media channels, including the Office of the Prime Minister of Ethiopia, regional communication bureaus, and media outlets such as Addis Standard, Tigray Television, and Ethiopian Broadcasting Corporation (EBC). His public presence is therefore institutional rather than personal.

column3: Sidama Region Communication Bureau (Facebook)
column9: Profile appears to match Desta Lendamo based on name and general presentation. However, identity cannot be fully confirmed due to lack of verification badge and limited publicly accessible information. Facebook privacy settings restrict full visibility, making attribution uncertain.

column2: President of Afar National Regional State
column3: Cross-reference from Facebook - X account linkage and Afar Regional State Communication Affairs Bureau
column9: X account is not verified but is directly referenced from the verified Facebook page, which increases reliability. It is considered highly likely to be authentic due to cross-platform linkage and consistent identity alignment with official communications.

column2: President of South Ethiopia Regional State
column3: South Ethiopia Regional State communication channels and Facebook page content (@TilahunKebedeGovernmentOfficial)
column9: Facebook page appears more consistent with official communication, sharing government updates and regional announcements aligned with the South Ethiopia Regional State administration. The X account cannot be confidently confirmed as official due to lack of verification badge, low follower count, unusually high following ratio, and long inactivity (around one year without posts). Overall, Facebook is treated as the more reliable channel, while the X account remains unconfirmed and requires further validation from official government sources.

column2: President of the Central Ethiopia Regional State
column3: Verified Facebook page (Endashawone) and Regional government communication channels
column9: The Facebook page is verified and appears to be the official communication channel for Endashaw Tassew, used for public updates and government-related announcements. No corresponding X (Twitter) account has been identified or confirmed.

column2: President of South West Ethiopia Peoples' Regional State
column3: Official Facebook page (negash.wagesho) and
column9: The Facebook page appears to be the primary official communication channel for Negash Wagesho, used for sharing government updates, development initiatives, and regional administrative announcements for the South West Ethiopia Peoples’ Region. No verified personal X account is identifiable official communication is instead conducted through government media and institutional channels.
